patches.find: The argument is robust either way: 3 seconds vs. the observed 138-second maximum is still a ~45× shortfall. A station within the penumbra band sees a partial eclipse for at most 60 seconds at the upper bound, or a few minutes at the slower mid-disc speed.
patches.replace: The argument is robust either way: 3 seconds vs. the observed 138-second maximum is still a ~45× shortfall. Even at the Moon’s actual observed angular speed (~0.25°/min at the dome’s published 2,534 km altitude), the dome geometry yields a totality duration of only ~7 seconds — still more than 20× short of the observed 138-second maximum — confirming the shortfall is structural, not an artefact of the assumed orbital speed. A station within the penumbra band sees a partial eclipse for at most 60 seconds at the upper bound, or a few minutes at the slower mid-disc speed.
patches.rationale: ISS-1742: Close the orbit-the-moon-even-slower escape route. EXP-272 math_verification derives ~7 sec from Moon angular speed ~0.25 deg/min at 2,534 km altitude and 1.3-km totality zone. Surfacing this in main prose pre-empts the defender wedge.

patches.find: well outside the dome’s own acceptance window) or revise the criterion
patches.replace: well outside the dome’s own falsification window) or revise the criterion
patches.rationale: ISS-1741/ISS-1704: acceptance window inverts the semantic. The ±3 nT envelope fires when measurement IS inside ±3 nT (falsification condition), not outside. falsification window matches the correct usage already used earlier in the paragraph.
